turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <radialGradient id="bh" cx="50%" cy="50%" r="55%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#020208"/>
      <stop offset="35%" stop-color="#070714"/>
      <stop offset="70%" stop-color="#0d0d22"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#150f2b"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <radialGradient id="ring" cx="50%" cy="50%" r="45%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#000" stop-opacity="0"/>
      <stop offset="55%" stop-color="#ffb74d" stop-opacity="0.65"/>
      <stop offset="75%" stop-color="#ff6f00" stop-opacity="0.25"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#000" stop-opacity="0"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <filter id="softGlow" x="-30%" y="-30%" width="160%" height="160%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="6" result="b"/>
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode in="b"/>
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/>
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- space background -->
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#050513"/>

  <!-- stars -->
  <g fill="#d7e2ff" opacity="0.95">
    <circle cx="78" cy="115" r="1.6"/>
    <circle cx="128" cy="70" r="1.2"/>
    <circle cx="190" cy="140" r="1.4"/>
    <circle cx="420" cy="120" r="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="465" cy="200" r="1.2"/>
    <circle cx="380" cy="70" r="1.1"/>
    <circle cx="55" cy="220" r="1.3"/>
    <circle cx="92" cy="320" r="1.2"/>
    <circle cx="170" cy="430" r="1.5"/>
    <circle cx="420" cy="360" r="1.4"/>
    <circle cx="460" cy="430" r="1.2"/>
    <circle cx="330" cy="445" r="1.1"/>
    <circle cx="250" cy="35" r="1.2"/>
    <circle cx="30" cy="400" r="1.1"/>
    <circle cx="500" cy="320" r="1.1"/>
    <circle cx="260" cy="270" r="1.0" opacity="0.6"/>
  </g>

  <!-- gravitational lens ring glow -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="256" r="120" fill="url(#ring)" filter="url(#softGlow)"/>

  <!-- accretion disk (subtle swirl) -->
  <g opacity="0.95" filter="url(#softGlow)">
    <ellipse cx="256" cy="256" rx="175" ry="70" fill="none" stroke="#ffcc80" stroke-opacity="0.35" stroke-width="10"/>
    <ellipse cx="256" cy="256" rx="140" ry="55" fill="none" stroke="#ff9800" stroke-opacity="0.28" stroke-width="8" transform="rotate(-12 256 256)"/>
    <ellipse cx="256" cy="256" rx="115" ry="45" fill="none" stroke="#ff6f00" stroke-opacity="0.25" stroke-width="7" transform="rotate(9 256 256)"/>

    <!-- spiral arcs -->
    <path d="M 165 245 C 210 165, 320 165, 355 250" fill="none" stroke="#ffd180" stroke-opacity="0.35" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M 155 270 C 205 210, 315 205, 365 275" fill="none" stroke="#ff8f00" stroke-opacity="0.28" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M 175 295 C 215 255, 295 245, 340 310" fill="none" stroke="#ff6f00" stroke-opacity="0.22" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  </g>

  <!-- event horizon -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="256" r="85" fill="url(#bh)"/>

  <!-- darker inner core and subtle rim -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="256" r="55" fill="#000" opacity="0.88"/>
  <circle cx="256" cy="256" r="88" fill="none" stroke="#2a1b52" stroke-opacity="0.9" stroke-width="6"/>
  <circle cx="256" cy="256" r="70" fill="none" stroke="#120a28" stroke-opacity="0.8" stroke-width="5"/>

  <!-- lensing warp: faint curved streaks around center -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#8fa6ff" stroke-opacity="0.15" stroke-width="2">
    <path d="M 85 260 C 135 200, 200 180, 260 180"/>
    <path d="M 110 330 C 160 275, 225 245, 290 235"/>
    <path d="M 120 205 C 180 175, 235 165, 305 175"/>
    <path d="M 95 345 C 155 310, 220 290, 305 285"/>
  </g>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <radialGradient id="bh" cx="50%" cy="50%" r="55%">

      <stop offset="35%" stop-color="#070714"/>

  </defs>

  <!-- space background -->

  <!-- stars -->

  </g>

  <!-- gravitational lens ring glow -->

  <!-- accretion disk (subtle swirl) -->
  <g opacity="0.95" filter="url(#softGlow)">
    <ellipse cx="256" cy="256" rx="175" ry="70" fill="none" stroke="#ffcc80" stroke-opacity="0.35" stroke-width="10"/>
    <ellipse cx="256" cy="256" rx="140" ry="55" fill="none" stroke="#ff9800" stroke-opacity="0.28" stroke-width="8" transform="rotate(-12 256 256)"/>

    <!-- spiral arcs -->

    <path d="M 155 270 C 205 210, 315 205, 365 275" fill="none" stroke="#ff8f00" stroke-opacity="0.28" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>

  </g>

  <!-- event horizon -->

  <circle cx="256" cy="256" r="88" fill="none" stroke="#2a1b52" stroke-opacity="0.9" stroke-width="6"/>

message.reasoning:
**Creating black hole visualization**

I need to design a concept of a black hole. I’m thinking maybe of a dark circle with an accretion disk, and I’ll add swirling lines, like a starry background with dots. I want to ensure there’s no text or numbers in the design. Using SVG, I picture a large black circle at the center with a gradient and a surrounding ring with rays and swirls. I aim for a simple, valid SVG in a 512x512 format.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
